channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4 million for allegedly taking drivers’ tips
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon wants to spend $100 billion this year for ‘once in a lifetime' AI opportunity
item.description: Amazon says it expects to spend in excess of $100 billion this year, with the “vast majority” going straight into AI projects.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: UK orders Apple to let it access everyone’s encrypted data
item.description: In its limited wisdom, the deeply unpopular UK government has decided to break privacy for the entire world, slamming Apple with a top secret order that demands blanket access to personal data. Apple must create a “back door” to enable surveillance, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump’s new government contractor rules
item.description: Like several other major tech companies, Google sells some of its technology and services to the federal government, including its rapidly growing cloud division that’s a key piece of its push into artificial technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Honeywell se transforma a partir de la IA generativa
item.description: Tras haber pasado años transformándose en una empresa totalmente basada en datos, el conglomerado estadounidense Honeywell está ahora preparado para aprovechar la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa en todas las facetas de su negocio para impulsar la mejora de la productividad, la colaboración y
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Repsol impulsa su estrategia digital con la incorporación de agentes de IA
item.description: Repsol ha anunciado la ampliación del acuerdo de colaboración suscrito con Accenture con el fin de continuar avanzando hacia los objetivos de su Programa Digital.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The number of venomous snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Not seeing ROI from your AI? Observability may be the missing link
item.description: From chatbots, to coding copilots, to AI agents, generative AI-powered apps are seeing increased traction among enterprises. As they go mainstream, however, their shortcomings are becoming more clear and problematic.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, Fox 5
